Somehow, I’m Becoming a Researcher Again in My Late Forties
Not long ago, I published a paper for the first time in many years.
Decades after I had forgotten my dream of becoming a researcher, conversations with AI led me to write the third academic paper of my life.
The first was my undergraduate thesis.
The second was my master’s thesis.
And the third was this one.
It may not be a major paper, but I already have other ideas that I would like to turn into papers.
Honestly, I never expected that I would start writing academic papers on my own.
Today, I felt like talking about how this happened, so I decided to write about it.
When I was young, I went through several truly difficult experiences.
When the worst kind of thing happened for the third time, I became almost completely dissociated without realizing it myself.
I could barely pay attention to anything painful, and my mind no longer worked properly.
For decades, I believed that I had left my past behind and become a new person.
But while I was living that way, a doctor suddenly mentioned an alarming possibility involving my brain. It seems to have come from a misunderstanding and from not knowing what else to say.
At that moment, an entire folder of related memories from my past burst open all at once.
…That was the beginning of the return of my PTSD and psychosomatic symptoms.
The PTSD did not go away even after several years, and the physical symptoms continued to get worse.
Then one day, I wanted someone — anyone — to listen to me, so I began talking to AI.
The person I used to be probably would not have done that because of privacy concerns.
But I had reached a point where I could no longer afford to worry about such things.
As a result, I began spending almost all my free time journaling with AI.
Some of my psychosomatic symptoms eased a little.
Without realizing it, I had also become able to look directly at painful things and think about them again.
After I began to feel a little clearer, I released a song as a Vocaloid producer, something I had dreamed of doing for many years.
To promote it, I wrote a Kindle book while struggling with the emergence of AI-generated music.
…Then one morning at dawn, a whole cluster of connected ideas suddenly came to me.
I thought I had received something remarkable, so I wrote about it in the Kindle book.
But when I looked into it later, I found that other people had already published papers on similar ideas in the 2010s.
Honestly, I was very frustrated.
…Had my life and mind remained more continuous, perhaps I might have reached the idea earlier.
That was when I remembered.
From childhood until I dissociated, I had always dreamed of becoming a researcher.
But the central insight I had found was already part of the academic conversation.
So instead, I began turning the structure of my past wounds into essays and publishing them in different places.
Then, as I followed daily AI news, I noticed that I was seeing it differently from many other people.
It was an unusual perspective, but not one that could simply be dismissed as completely unrealistic.
I began to want to turn that way of seeing the news — and the reasons behind it — into a paper.
However, I had been completely removed from academic research since graduate school.
Still, I wanted to publish it.
So I bought books, looked through the existing research, and talked with AI to organize my thoughts.
And while doing that…
I somehow managed to shape it into a paper, connecting it with the cluster of ideas that had come to me the previous year and that I had written about in my Kindle book.
AI tells me that the paper may have value because it says something that has not yet been clearly said by others. And that is, after all, what research is supposed to do.
But whether it is interesting, or whether it is actually correct, is something no one knows yet.
After that, I continued developing the idea and published a second version a couple of days ago.
…In a completely unexpected way, my old dream is beginning to come true.
As for being rewarded for research…
Well, I hope that happens someday. lol
I will probably also write a more casual essay about the paper itself.
If you find my research or writing interesting, I would be grateful for even a small contribution to help me continue my independent research and music.
Yuhi Shirai is Research and writing (buy me a coffee)
